title: Link DataTools Pro Users to PostHog Person Records
description:
Implement PostHog person identification to link DataTools Pro users to PostHog person records. When a DTP user signs up or logs in, the PostHog JavaScript SDK should be called to declare the DTP User ID (the full GUID identifier sent to Salesforce, not the auto-increment ID).

This creates a connection between PostHog analytics data and DTP user records, allowing:
- Viewing that a PostHog person is a DataTools Pro user
- Seeing their DTP app identifier within the PostHog person profile
- Correlating product analytics events with specific user accounts across PostHog and Salesforce
acceptance_criteria: PostHog person records include the DTP User GUID identifier
The identifier matches what is sent to Salesforce (not the auto-increment ID)
PostHog JS identify call is triggered on user sign-up or login
Person records in PostHog show the DTP user association
Existing PostHog events can be correlated to identified DTP users
middleware_changes: Add PostHog JS identify call on user authentication to send the DTP User GUID as the person identifier. Ensure the GUID (not auto-increment ID) is used for cross-system consistency with Salesforce.
implementation_notes: Use the PostHog JS SDK posthog.identify() method with the DTP User GUID. The GUID must be the same identifier stored in Salesforce for cross-platform analytics consistency.
